titleStmt.title: Paper Agreed Upon by the French, United Kingdom, and United States Delegations to the United Nations

div.note: CFM Files: Lot M–88: Box 152: SFM Documents 1–40

head.hi: Paper Agreed Upon by the French, United Kingdom, and United States Delegations to the United Nations
head.note: Attached to the source text was a cover sheet, not printed, dated September 7.

p.hi: Position Concerning Draft Resolution on Spain

div.p:
In preliminary discussions with the U.K., French and U.S. Delegations in New York on August 25 and September 6 it was agreed that 1.
The three Governments would not support a resolution in the GA which would imply approval of the present Spanish Government or repeal in its entirety the 1946 Resolution of the GA which recommended recall of ambassadors, barred Spain from the Specialized Agencies and other UN activities, and referred to the origin and character of the Spanish Government and to possible future SC action.
2.
The draft resolution proposed by Peru and Bolivia is likewise unacceptable.

p.hi: Question for Discussion by the Foreign Ministers

div.p: The U.S. indicated its intention to support an amendment to the 1946 Resolution which would revoke the two recommendations that members recall their Ambassadors and that Spain be barred from membership in the Specialized Agencies. The U.K. and French Delegations reserved their positions.
